title: tephrocactus articulatus
intro: Tephrocactus articulatus, also known as the segmented prickly pear cactus, is a type of cactus that belongs to the Echinocactaceae family. This plant is native to the southwestern region of the United States and Mexico, and its stems and flowers are used in traditional medicine. The medicinal properties of Tephrocactus articulatus include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The health benefits of this plant include improving skin health, reducing inflammation, and boosting the immune system, and it can be used in herbal preparations such as salves, ointments, and infusions.
